Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

That "NFS specific error code" is also used by afs, btrfs, smb, squashfs, ubifs and a huge amount of code in drivers/
So I think the "is not a standard error code and should be avoided in new patches." in checkpatch.pl is trying to close the gate after the horse has bolted.

But if you are certain that no external use-space code depends on ENOTSUPP (and it would seem unlikely that any code does), then I guess we may as well make the change.  It might ease the upstreaming path.

Patch Set 1:

The main reason to make the change is that ENOTSUPP does not generate a sensible error message if it ever makes it out to userspace.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> The main reason to make the change is that ENOTSUPP does not generate a sensible error message if it ever makes it out to userspace.

That is an excellent reason.  It wouldn't hurt at all to put in the commit message :-)
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

parents.subject: LU-15626 tests: Remove variables not used

commit.subject: LU-14301 client: use EOPNOTSUPP instead of ENOTSUPP
commit.message:
LU-14301 client: use EOPNOTSUPP instead of ENOTSUPP

Don't return NFS-specific error code ENOTSUPP back to userspace,
instead use EOPNOTSUPP.  ENOTSUPP does not print a useful error
message from strerror() if it is hit by an application.

Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Iabd07b31069737e8ee7ca2382fd8cff6143ebbe5
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51511
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Neil Brown <neilb@suse.de>
Reviewed-by: jsimmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
